policy_statement: Adopted a community climate protection goal to become carbon neutral by 2050 and directed creation of a plan to meet the goal (Resolution 31312).
verbatim_text: In 2011, the Mayor and City Council adopted a bold climate protection goal for our community to become carbon neutral by 2050 and directed the creation of a plan to meet the goal (Resolution 31312).
extraction_rationale: Contains an explicit, formal goal (carbon neutral by 2050) adopted by Mayor and City Council and a directive via a named resolution.
reasoning: The statement includes a clear, measurable outcome (“become carbon neutral”) with a specific deadline (“by 2050”), and it is tied to formal city action via a named resolution. It also includes a directive to create a plan, but the primary commitment is the carbon-neutral-by-2050 goal. Therefore it meets the strict criteria for an individual policy (quantifiable target + timeline), and the resolution provides a binding/mechanistic basis for the commitment.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text states an outcome that can be measured at the community level: "become carbon neutral". Timeline: It includes an explicit deadline: "by 2050". Binding mechanism: It references formal adoption and an official instrument: "the Mayor and City Council adopted" and "(Resolution 31312)", plus an implementation directive: "directed the creation of a plan". Spatial specificity: Applies to the jurisdiction/community referenced throughout: "for our community". Weak language: Not primarily framed as "encourage/explore"; it is an adopted goal with a directive. Strong language: Uses decisive verbs tied to the commitment: "adopted ... goal" and "directed the creation of a plan" along with the hard date "by 2050". STRICT VALID RULE check: (1) Auditable commitment present (carbon neutral goal), (2) Gold standard satisfied (timeline Yes + quantifiable target Yes), (3) Language strength satisfied because weak language is No and there is a formal resolution-based directive.

policy_statement: Amend the Seattle Electrical Code to require new parking to be built with electrical capacity and documented conduit/equipment locations to facilitate EV charging at one charging station per parking space.
verbatim_text: In 2017, the Seattle Electrical Code was amended to ensure new parking is built to facilitate future electric vehicle charging infrastructure. It includes an explicit year (“In 2017”), satisfying timeline. Language is strong (“was amended,” “requires”). Therefore it meets the strict validity rule.

Timeline: "In 2017, the Seattle Electrical Code was amended" provides an explicit year. Binding mechanism: "Seattle Electrical Code was amended" and "The code requires..." indicate an enforceable code requirement. Spatial specificity: Applies to "Seattle" and to "new parking" within that jurisdiction. Weak language: No terms like encourage/explore/aim appear; requirements are stated directly ("requires"). Strong language: "was amended" and "requires" are directive verbs tied to a hard numeric standard ("one ... per parking space"). Under the strict rule, it is auditable, has both timeline and quantifiable target, and uses strong/binding language, so VALID.
